title: TCP active/passive mode and controlling process
purpose: BEAM TCP sockets have two receive modes: passive (the process calls recv explicitly) and active (data arrives as messages in the process mailbox automatically). Active mode is essential for OTP's gen_tcp and gen_server integration — servers receive {tcp, Socket, Data} messages in their receive loop. This brief adds the active/passive mode switch and the controlling process concept (only the controlling process receives active-mode messages). Active mode requires a background read loop that submits reads to the ring and delivers completions as messages.
task: Add active/passive mode state to FdResource. In active mode, a background read loop submits continuous reads and delivers data as {tcp, FdResource, Binary} messages to the controlling process. Implement setopts for mode switching and controlling_process for ownership transfer.

requirements.title: Socket mode state on FdResource
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add mode state to FdInner: active (true/false/once) and controlling_process (pid). WHEN a TCP socket is created, THE SYSTEM SHALL default to passive mode with the creating process as controlling process.
acceptance: FdInner gains mode: AtomicU8 (Passive=0, Active=1, ActiveOnce=2) and controlling_process: AtomicU64
Default: Passive mode, controlling_process = creating process PID
Accessor methods: mode(), controlling_process(), set_mode(), set_controlling_process()

requirements.title: Active mode read loop
requirements.spec: WHEN a socket is set to active mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a continuous read to the CompletionRing. WHEN data arrives, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver {tcp, SocketTerm, DataBinary} as a message to the controlling process and resubmit the read. WHEN the connection closes, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver {tcp_closed, SocketTerm}. WHEN an error occurs, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver {tcp_error, SocketTerm, Reason}.
acceptance: Active mode transitions trigger a read submission to the ring
On read completion: build {tcp, Socket, Data} tuple, enqueue in controlling process mailbox
Active mode: resubmit read after each delivery (continuous)
Active-once mode: deliver one message, then switch to passive (no resubmit)
Connection close (0 bytes): deliver {tcp_closed, Socket}, switch to passive
Error: deliver {tcp_error, Socket, Reason}, switch to passive
Test: set socket active, send data from peer, verify {tcp, _, Data} message received

requirements.title: setopts and controlling_process BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide BIFs for setting socket options (active/passive mode) and transferring the controlling process.
acceptance: Switching from passive to active starts the read loop
Switching from active to passive stops resubmitting reads (in-flight read completes normally)
Only the current controlling process can transfer control
Non-controlling process calling this returns {error, not_owner}
Test: set active once, receive one message, verify socket reverts to passive

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement {active, N} (OTP 25+ flow control) — future enhancement
SHALL NOT implement packet mode ({packet, N}) — future enhancement
SHALL NOT implement binary vs list mode — all data delivered as binaries
verification: cargo test passes with active mode message delivery
Active-once delivers exactly one message then reverts to passive
Controlling process transfer works between processes
Connection close in active mode delivers tcp_closed message
Mode switch during in-flight read does not crash or lose data
